actor_actions: User begins a drag (dragstart) on a product li element that has draggable="true"
constraints: Capture must occur during the drag (e.g., dragstart) so the title and price are available for transfer via the browser DataTransfer API
The dragged element must be a product li element (see UI anchors)
expected_observables: The drag event's DataTransfer is populated with the product's title and the product's price (both values captured for transfer)
preconditions: Product list is rendered and the user can interact with product items

source_evidence.quote: The drag event should capture the title and price of the product being dragged.

actor_actions: User views or attempts to drag a product item
constraints: Product item structure must include image, title, and price nodes and expose data-testid attributes such as 'product-item-N', 'product-title-N', and 'product-price-N'
expected_observables: Each product is represented by an li element with draggable="true" and contains an image, a title element, and a price element identifiable by data-testid attributes (example patterns shown)
preconditions: Product list is rendered

source_evidence.quote: Each product item contains an image, a title, and a price, identified by data-testid attributes such as 'product-item-1', 'product-title-1', and 'product-price-1'.

actor_actions: User drags a product item (e.g. element with data-testid="product-item-1") and drops it onto the designated drop area (id="div1" / data-testid="drop-area").
constraints: The add action is triggered by dragging a product item into the element identified by id="div1" or data-testid="drop-area".
expected_observables: A new cart entry for the dropped product is displayed after the drop (visible in the cart UI area).
preconditions: Product list is rendered and contains one or more product items with data-testid attributes (e.g. data-testid="product-item-1").
Designated drop area is present (id="div1", data-testid="drop-area").

source_evidence.quote: drag and drop product items(data-testid="product-item-1", data-testid="product-item-2", etc.) into a designated drop area (id="div1", data-testid="drop-area") to add them to the cart.

state_effects: Cart state contains an entry for the dropped product.

actor_actions: User drops a product item into the designated drop area.
constraints: The product title must appear specifically in the element(s) matching selector .box2.
expected_observables: The dropped product's title is visible in the cart UI at selector .box2.
preconditions: A product item has been dropped into the designated drop area (see add_item_on_drop).

source_evidence.quote: the product title appears in .box2, the unit price in .box3, and the quantity in .box1.

state_effects: Cart display includes the product title mapped to .box2 for the added item.

actor_actions: User drops a product item into the designated drop area.
constraints: The unit price must be presented specifically in element(s) matching selector .box3.
expected_observables: The dropped product's unit price is visible in the cart UI at selector .box3.
preconditions: A product item has been dropped into the designated drop area.

source_evidence.quote: the product title appears in .box2, the unit price in .box3, and the quantity in .box1.

state_effects: Cart display includes the product's unit price mapped to .box3 for the added item.

actor_actions: User drags and drops the same product item again into the designated drop area.
constraints: Repeated adds of the same product must update the quantity visible in .box1 rather than creating a duplicate unrelated entry.
expected_observables: The quantity shown for that product increases in the cart display at selector .box1 (reflecting the repeated add).
preconditions: A given product has already been added to the cart (one or more entries visible).

source_evidence.quote: The system supports adding both different and repeated products.

state_effects: Cart quantity for the repeated product increments accordingly (single observable: .box1 updated).

actor_actions: User drags and drops two different product items into the designated drop area (id="div1" / data-testid="drop-area").
constraints: Different products dropped must result in distinct cart entries, each mapping title->.box2, price->.box3, quantity->.box1.
expected_observables: Both different products appear in the cart display (separate entries), each showing their title in .box2, price in .box3, and quantity in .box1.
preconditions: Multiple distinct product items are available in the product list (e.g. data-testid="product-item-1", data-testid="product-item-2").

source_evidence.quote: The system supports adding both different and repeated products.

state_effects: Cart state contains distinct entries for each different product added.

actor_actions: Drag one or more products from the product list and drop them into the cart area
constraints: Total must equal the arithmetic sum of item prices
Total must include the appropriate currency symbol
Total must be formatted to two decimal places
Total must be displayed inside <div id="allMoney">
expected_observables: The <div id="allMoney"> element's text equals the numeric sum of the dropped product prices formatted with the currency symbol and two decimal places (example: "$80.00")
preconditions: Product list is visible with predefined product prices
Cart drop area is present

source_evidence.quote: The system calculates the total price by summing the prices of all products in the cart

state_effects: Cart contains the dropped products
Displayed total reflects the sum of the cart's product prices

actor_actions: Add product(s) whose prices sum to a whole-dollar value into the cart
constraints: Always display exactly two digits after the decimal point
Include the appropriate currency symbol
expected_observables: The <div id="allMoney"> element shows two decimal places including trailing zeros (example: "$80.00")
preconditions: Product prices chosen sum to an integer-dollar amount (no cents)

source_evidence.quote: formatting the total to two decimal places with the appropriate currency symbol (e.g., '$80.00')

state_effects: Displayed total uses fixed two-decimal formatting even for whole-dollar totals

actor_actions: Add product(s) whose prices produce a fractional-cent total into the cart
constraints: Final displayed total must be presented with exactly two decimal places
Currency symbol must be present alongside the rounded/formatted value
expected_observables: The <div id="allMoney"> element shows the total rounded/formatted to two decimal places with the currency symbol (for example, a computed 19.995 displays as "$20.00")
preconditions: Product prices chosen result in a sum with more than two decimal digits (e.g., fractional cents)

source_evidence.quote: formatting the total to two decimal places

state_effects: Displayed total is the two-decimal representation of the numeric sum

actor_actions: Click the Add note button (id="add", class="add", data-testid="add-note-button")
constraints: The created element must be a <div> whose class attribute includes "note" and it must be appended to document.body (not only elsewhere in the DOM)
expected_observables: A new <div> element with class "note" is added as a child of the document body
preconditions: Application is loaded and the Add note button is present (id="add", class="add", data-testid="add-note-button")

source_evidence.quote: a new note is created and appended to the document body as a <div> element with the class "note".

state_effects: Document body contains one additional element matching div.note

actor_actions: Inspect the content of the newly created note element
constraints: The textarea must be visible to the user and contain zero-length content (empty)
expected_observables: Inside the note there is a visible <textarea> element with data-testid="note-textarea" and its value/text is empty
preconditions: A note element (div.note) exists after activating Add note

source_evidence.quote: Inside each note, there is a visible, empty <textarea> (data-testid="note-textarea") for user input.

state_effects: The note contains an empty textarea ready for user input

actor_actions: Click the Add note button multiple times in rapid succession
constraints: Each click must produce a distinct note element (no replacement of existing notes); each created note must include its own textarea element
expected_observables: For each rapid click, a separate div.note is created and appended to document.body; the count of div.note increases by the number of clicks and each created note includes its own textarea[data-testid="note-textarea"]
preconditions: Application is loaded and the Add note button is present (id="add", class="add", data-testid="add-note-button")

source_evidence.quote: The application supports multiple rapid clicks on the "Add note" button, with each click creating a separate note, each containing its own textarea.

state_effects: Multiple distinct note elements exist in the DOM, each containing its own textarea

actor_actions: User types Markdown text into the textarea (data-testid="note-textarea")
constraints: Rendering must occur in real-time as characters are entered (no manual toggle or save required to see updates)
This behavior addresses user-observable updates only (DOM rendering), not verification of underlying Markdown library calls
expected_observables: The main display area (data-testid="note-main") updates continuously as the user types, showing rendered HTML corresponding to the Markdown input (real-time update)
preconditions: The note is in edit mode and the textarea (data-testid="note-textarea") is visible and focused

source_evidence.quote: As the user types Markdown (e.g., # Heading, **bold**, _italic_, [link](...)), the content is dynamically rendered in the main display area (data-testid="note-main") in real-time, with proper HTML formatting (e.g., **Bold** becomes Bold).

state_effects: Visible content of note-main reflects latest typed Markdown without requiring an explicit save

actor_actions: User enters inline Markdown such as **Bold** or _italic_ into the textarea
constraints: Rendered output must show HTML formatting rather than escaped literal Markdown for supported inline syntax
expected_observables: The main display area (data-testid="note-main") renders the corresponding HTML tags for inline styles (e.g., **Bold** appears as Bold)
preconditions: The note is in edit mode with the textarea (data-testid="note-textarea") visible

source_evidence.quote: with proper HTML formatting (e.g., **Bold** becomes Bold)

state_effects: Displayed note content contains HTML elements representing the Markdown semantics

actor_actions: User exits edit mode by clicking the Edit button (data-testid="edit-note-button")
constraints: If the Markdown is invalid (e.g., unmatched symbols), the app must not crash or show an unhandled error; it should display the raw input on exit as the user-observable outcome
expected_observables: The main display area (data-testid="note-main") shows the raw input text (verbatim), and the app remains responsive (no crash or unhandled error visible to the user)
preconditions: The textarea (data-testid="note-textarea") contains invalid or unmatched Markdown syntax (e.g., an unmatched **)

source_evidence.quote: invalid Markdown (e.g., unmatched **) does not cause crashes or errors, and instead displays the raw input when the user exits the edit mode by clicking the "Edit" button (data-testid="edit-note-button").

state_effects: Displayed note content reflects the raw/unchanged input when exiting edit mode with invalid Markdown

actor_actions: User modifies the note content by typing or deleting characters in the textarea
constraints: This behavior concerns observable storage state; it does not assert internal implementation details beyond the presence of updated content in localStorage
expected_observables: localStorage reflects the current state of the note immediately after changes (the stored note content matches the latest textarea content without requiring an explicit save)
preconditions: A note is being edited in the textarea (data-testid="note-textarea")

source_evidence.quote: All changes should be reflected immediately in local storage to persist the current state of the note.

state_effects: localStorage keys representing the note contain the updated content corresponding to the latest edits

actor_actions: Click the Add note button (data-testid="add-note-button")
constraints: Transition to edit mode must occur immediately upon creation
expected_observables: The textarea (data-testid="note-textarea") is visible for user input
The main content area (data-testid="note-main") is hidden
preconditions: Application page is loaded

source_evidence.quote: Upon creation, the note should immediately enter edit mode: the textarea (data-testid="note-textarea") is visible for user input, while the main content area (data-testid="note-main") remains hidden.

state_effects: A new note is created and its UI state is set to edit mode

actor_actions: Click the Edit button (data-testid="edit-note-button") to switch to view mode
constraints: Clicking the Edit button toggles the UI into view mode where the main content is shown and the textarea is hidden
expected_observables: The main content area (data-testid="note-main") is visible displaying formatted content
The textarea (data-testid="note-textarea") is hidden
preconditions: A note exists (for example, currently in edit mode)

source_evidence.quote: Users can switch between edit and view modes by clicking the "Edit" button (data-testid="edit-note-button"). In view mode, the main content area becomes visible to display the formatted content, and the textarea is hidden; in edit mode, this behavior is reversed.

state_effects: The note's UI state changes to view mode

actor_actions: Click the Edit button (data-testid="edit-note-button") to switch to edit mode
constraints: Clicking the Edit button toggles the UI back into edit mode where the textarea is shown and the main content is hidden
expected_observables: The textarea (data-testid="note-textarea") is visible for user input
The main content area (data-testid="note-main") is hidden
preconditions: A note exists and is currently in view mode

source_evidence.quote: In view mode, the main content area becomes visible to display the formatted content, and the textarea is hidden; in edit mode, this behavior is reversed.

state_effects: The note's UI state changes to edit mode

actor_actions: Type or modify text inside the textarea (data-testid="note-textarea")
constraints: Saves must be automatic (no explicit save button) and preserve state across sessions
expected_observables: Local storage is updated automatically with the note's latest text
After a page reload, the note's content reflects the last typed changes
preconditions: A note exists and the textarea (data-testid="note-textarea") is visible (edit mode)

source_evidence.quote: All changes typed into the textarea should be automatically saved to local storage, preserving the note’s state across sessions.

state_effects: The note's persisted representation in localStorage is updated to include the typed changes

actor_actions: Click the note's delete button (data-testid="delete-note-button")
constraints: Removal must occur immediately upon the click (the note must no longer appear on the page after the click)
expected_observables: The targeted note's DOM node is no longer present on the page immediately after the click
preconditions: A note is present in the page and has an associated delete button with data-testid="delete-note-button"

source_evidence.quote: must be immediately removed from the DOM so it no longer appears on the page

state_effects: No visual trace of the deleted note remains in the DOM
ui_anchors: the note container element that previously displayed the note text

actor_actions: Click the note's delete button (data-testid="delete-note-button")
constraints: The removal from localStorage must be a consequence of the same delete click action (no separate manual persistence step required)
expected_observables: The deleted note's entry is removed from the stored notes array in localStorage (the note is no longer present in localStorage data)
preconditions: The note to be deleted exists as an entry in the application's stored notes array in localStorage

source_evidence.quote: the corresponding entry must also be removed from localStorage

state_effects: localStorage no longer contains the deleted note's entry in the stored notes array

actor_actions: User opens / reloads the Notes App webpage
constraints: Rendering must occur immediately after retrieval on page load; creation of DOM nodes is dynamic per stored entry
expected_observables: The application displays one rendered note element for each entry in the stored notes array
Rendered notes show their content (see behavior 5.3) or editing UI (see behavior 5.4) as appropriate
preconditions: localStorage contains a JSON-serialized array stored under the key 'notes'

source_evidence.quote: When the Notes App webpage loads, it must retrieve all saved notes from localStorage and display them accordingly.

state_effects: The internal notes variable is populated from localStorage by parsing JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('notes'))

actor_actions: The app renders a stored note into the page (e.g., on page load)
constraints: The specified data-testid attributes and class names must be present for each stored note element
expected_observables: Each rendered note is a div element with class 'note'
Inside each .note there is a div.tools containing an "Edit" button (data-testid="edit-note-button") and a "Delete" button (data-testid="delete-note-button")
Each .note contains a div.main (data-testid="note-main") and a textarea (data-testid="note-textarea")
preconditions: A stored notes array contains at least one entry

source_evidence.quote: For each stored note, the app should dynamically create a div element with the class note, which includes a div with the class tools containing an "Edit" button (data-testid="edit-note-button") and a "Delete" button (data-testid="delete-note-button").

state_effects: A DOM node per stored note is created with the required sub-elements

actor_actions: The app renders the stored non-empty note (e.g., on page load)
constraints: Visibility semantics (visible vs hidden) must reflect the default display state described; the behavior does not mandate how visibility is implemented (CSS class, hidden attribute, inline style, etc.)
expected_observables: The note's div.main (data-testid="note-main") is visible and shows the note text
The note's textarea (data-testid="note-textarea") exists but is hidden by default
preconditions: A stored note entry contains non-empty content (non-empty string) in the notes array

source_evidence.quote: Notes retrieved from localStorage should be rendered with their content displayed in the main div, with the textarea hidden by default.

state_effects: Rendered note's display mode is set to show the main view and keep the textarea hidden

actor_actions: The app renders the stored empty note (e.g., on page load)
constraints: The requirement only specifies that empty-content notes show the textarea and hide the main view; do not assume specific placeholder text or focus behavior
expected_observables: The note's textarea (data-testid="note-textarea") is shown to allow immediate editing
The note's div.main (data-testid="note-main") is hidden
preconditions: A stored note entry has no content (the requirement describes this case as 'no content')

source_evidence.quote: For notes that have no content, the application should instead show the textarea and hide the main div, allowing immediate editing.

state_effects: Rendered note's display mode is set to editing with textarea visible

actor_actions: User opens / reloads the Notes App webpage
constraints: The app must not render placeholder note elements when no notes are present; the requirement does not demand rendering of alternative UI (e.g., 'no notes' messaging)
expected_observables: No .note elements are present in the DOM
preconditions: localStorage does not contain a 'notes' entry or the stored value is empty such that there are no saved notes

source_evidence.quote: If localStorage is empty, no notes should be shown.

state_effects: The internal notes variable is empty or absent after attempting JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('notes'))
